name: Flow Metrics
description: Compute Flow Framework / DORA-style metrics (cycle time, lead time, throughput, WIP, flow load, rework rate, flow efficiency, flow distribution, defect ratio) over a Jira project / team / Jira Align program / portfolio scope and an inclusive time window. Read-only: composes the `jira` skill (and `jira-align` for program / portfolio scope) by name, emits canonical JSON / CSV / per-issue JSONL, and never issues a write verb against either tracker. v1 is stdlib only — percentiles use `statistics.quantiles(..., method="exclusive")`, no numpy.

deps._runtime_contract: Sibling skills are addressed BY NAME at runtime. The agent's harness resolves the name to whatever install location the user picked. `source` below is install guidance only — never a runtime path.

skills.name: jira
skills.purpose: All Jira reads: check, whoami, get-issue, search, get-project, plus a tightly-allowlisted set of `raw GET` paths (field catalog, project statuses, per-issue changelog pagination). flow-metrics never makes a raw Jira REST call and never invokes a write verb (no transition, comment, attach, create / update / delete).

skills.name: jira-align
skills.purpose: Used only for --program-id / --portfolio-id scope, and only for the four nested-resource `raw GET` paths: programs/<id>, programs/<id>/teams, portfolios/<id>, portfolios/<id>/programs. Time-in-state numbers always come from Jira's changelog; jira-align is exclusively for team / program / portfolio membership resolution.

deps._pip_rationale: No deps.pip entry: v1 is stdlib only. Percentiles use statistics.quantiles(..., method="exclusive") at indices 49 / 74 / 89 for p50 / p75 / p90 — equivalent to numpy.percentile(..., method="linear") on the spec's sample sizes. The output schema validator is the same stdlib-only posture; no jsonschema dependency. A future v2 that adopts numpy would add deps.pip then.

input.arguments: Required: exactly one of --project KEY / --program-id ID / --portfolio-id ID. Optional: --team NAME (only valid with --project); --from YYYY-MM-DD / --to YYYY-MM-DD (window bounds, both inclusive, default: last 90 days ending today UTC); --jql "<expr>" (extra JQL ANDed into the scope query, always parenthesised); --align-filter "<expr>" (extra OData ANDed into Jira Align queries); --cohort-jql "<expr>" (marks matching issues with cohort: true and emits a cohort_breakdown block in aggregate mode); --metrics LIST (comma list from cycle_time, lead_time, throughput, wip, flow_load, rework_rate, flow_time, flow_efficiency, flow_distribution, defect_ratio; default: all); --state-config FILE (default: references/states.default.json); --issuetype-config FILE (default: references/issuetypes.default.json); --team-field-override ID; --align-join-field NAME (required for Align scope unless set in the state config); --align-teams-path PATH (one of the four allowlisted jira-align patterns); --include-subtasks; --format json|csv; --output FILE (required for --per-issue); --per-issue (one JSONL row per issue); --no-cache; --verbose; --yes (overwrite --output without prompting).

env._note: v1 honours no environment-variable overrides — every input is a CLI flag. Cache directory is fixed at .context/flow-metrics/cache/ under cwd (created mode 0700). A future v2 may add a FLOW_METRICS_CACHE_DIR override; not present today.

artifacts: stdout (default) or --output FILE: canonical JSON aggregate report (or CSV long-form when --format csv).
--per-issue --output FILE: per-issue JSONL, one row per in-scope issue, sorted by key codepoint.
.context/flow-metrics/cache/<cache-key>.jsonl: per-issue derivation cache (atomic write via .tmp + os.replace). Bypassed by --no-cache.

meta.caller, meta.sources, meta.metrics_requested, meta.cohort_jql (when --cohort-jql is set), meta.per_team_double_counted, meta.state_config_sha, meta.issuetype_config_sha, meta.window, meta.generated_at, meta.schema_version recorded in the JSON output's meta block.
